question.text: Under SEBI (CAPSM) Regulations, 2007, what transition provision existed for associated persons already employed before the specified date?

answer.text: They could continue to be employed if they obtained the certificate within two years from the specified date
They were permanently exempted from certification requirements
They had to obtain the certificate within 6 months
They were required to stop working until they obtained the certificate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the SEBI (CAPSM) Regulations, 2007, an associated person employed or engaged by an intermediary prior to the date specified by SEBI could continue to be employed if he obtained the certificate within two years from the said date. **The Trap:** The provision was not a permanent exemption but a time-limited grace period of 2 years. After the grace period, certification became mandatory. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This transition provision balanced operational continuity with regulatory compliance — allowing time for existing professionals to obtain certification without disrupting intermediary operations.
